source.title: IMpower010 — ctDNA and adjuvant atezolizumab

finding.description: ctDNA clearance post-surgery predicts benefit from adjuvant atezolizumab in stage IB-IIIA NSCLC.
finding.effect_summary: ctDNA-positive patients had significantly improved DFS with adjuvant immunotherapy vs. BSC

source.title: TRACERx — ultrasensitive ctDNA in NSCLC

finding.description: TRACERx demonstrated ultrasensitive ctDNA detection using PhasED-Seq with 197 patients and 1,069 samples.
finding.effect_summary: 197 pts, 1,069 samples analyzed

source_excerpt: Here we developed ctDNA methods tracking a median of 200 mutations identified in resected NSCLC tissue across 1,069 plasma samples collected from 197 patients enrolled in the TRACERx study.

source.title: TRACERx — ultrasensitive ctDNA in NSCLC

finding.description: PhasED-Seq achieved LOD95 of 1 ppm versus 84 ppm for CAPP-Seq.
finding.effect_summary: LOD95 1 ppm vs. 84 ppm CAPP-Seq

source_excerpt: "Analytical and orthogonal validation of variant DNA detection using the locked-assay was performed (Supplementary Note). 659 spike-in samples were analysed at assay DNA inputs of 2ng to 80ng and variant DNA levels of 0.003% to 0.1% (methods). Sensitivity for variant DNA detection using a 50-variant PSP at 0.01% variant DNA level (representative of ctDNA levels encountered post-resection of NSCLC, using current MRD assays 8) was >90% at DNA inputs of 20ng and above."

source.title: TRACERx — ultrasensitive ctDNA in NSCLC

finding.description: PhasED-Seq achieved clinical sensitivity of 67% versus 28% for CAPP-Seq.
finding.effect_summary: clinical sensitivity 67% vs. 28%, P=0.022

source.title: TRACERx — ultrasensitive ctDNA in NSCLC

finding.description: Median lead time of 164 days before clinical relapse was achieved with PhasED-Seq.
finding.effect_summary: median lead time of 164 days before clinical relapse

source_excerpt:
Based on my careful review of the entire paper, I found no mention of "PhasED-Seq" or a median lead time of 164 days before clinical relapse. The paper focuses on ctDNA tracking using AMP (Amplicon-based sequencing) methodology and ECLIPSE bioinformatic tool, but does not discuss PhasED-Seq.

NO_MATCH

source.title: AEGEAN exploratory analysis — ASCO 2025

finding.description: Patients without ctDNA clearance during neoadjuvant treatment or with post-surgical MRD had worse outcomes with perioperative durvalumab.
finding.effect_summary: worse outcomes without ctDNA clearance or with post-surgical MRD

source.title: DART trial — ASCO 2025

finding.description: Detectable ctDNA during consolidative durvalumab predicts progression 7.4 months before radiological evidence.
finding.effect_summary: predicts progression 7.4 months before radiological evidence

source.title: NSCLC ctDNA meta-analysis — JCO Precision Oncology 2025

finding.description: A meta-analysis of 13 studies with 1,309 patients confirmed longitudinal ctDNA monitoring as strongest prognostic signal.
finding.effect_summary: HR 8.70, strongest prognostic signal

source.title: NCCN NSCLC Guidelines v3.2025

finding.description: NCCN NSCLC Guidelines reference ctDNA as an emerging tool but do not yet include a specific MRD recommendation.
finding.effect_summary: emerging tool but no specific MRD recommendation

source.title: TRACERx — lead time in NSCLC

finding.description: TRACERx showed median 164-day lead time with ultrasensitive PhasED-Seq assay.
finding.effect_summary: median 164-day lead time with ultrasensitive PhasED-Seq

source_excerpt: "We developed PhasED-Seq 12 , 31 , a cost-effective molecular barcoding technology targeting 16-96 mutations, which detected MRD with a median lead time of 164 days."

source.title: TRACERx — Signatera validation in NSCLC

finding.description: PhasED-Seq achieves LOD95 of 1 ppm with 67% clinical sensitivity versus 28% for standard SNV-based methods.
finding.effect_summary: LOD95 1 ppm, 67% sensitivity vs. 28% standard methods

source_excerpt:
Looking through the paper for information about PhasED-Seq, LOD95, and clinical sensitivity comparisons...

NO_MATCH

source.title: Potential of Minimal Residual Disease in Guiding Adjuvant Therapy Decisions in Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer.

finding.description: 165 patients with stage IA to IIIB NSCLC who had undergone confirmed R0 resection were enrolled with blood samples collected 1 month after surgery before initiation of adjuvant therapy
finding.effect_summary: Study enrolled 165 patients with stage IA-IIIB NSCLC post-R0 resection

source.title: Potential of Minimal Residual Disease in Guiding Adjuvant Therapy Decisions in Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer.

finding.description: 35 out of 165 patients experienced relapses
finding.effect_summary: 21.2% relapse rate in post-surgical NSCLC patients

source.title: Potential of Minimal Residual Disease in Guiding Adjuvant Therapy Decisions in Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer.

finding.description: Positive MRD at landmark was associated with shorter disease-free survival than negative MRD
finding.effect_summary: Positive MRD associated with 12-fold increased risk of recurrence (HR=12.0, P<0.001)

source.title: Potential of Minimal Residual Disease in Guiding Adjuvant Therapy Decisions in Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer.

finding.description: In patients with negative landmark MRD and stage IB with high-risk factors, there was no significant difference in DFS between those who received adjuvant therapy and those who did not
finding.effect_summary: No DFS benefit from adjuvant therapy in MRD-negative stage IB high-risk patients (P=0.974)

source.title: Potential of Minimal Residual Disease in Guiding Adjuvant Therapy Decisions in Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer.

finding.description: In patients with negative landmark MRD and EGFR mutations, there was no significant difference in DFS between those with or without adjuvant chemotherapy before adjuvant targeted therapy
finding.effect_summary: No DFS benefit from adjuvant chemotherapy in MRD-negative EGFR-mutant patients (P=0.502)

source.title: Potential of Minimal Residual Disease in Guiding Adjuvant Therapy Decisions in Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer.

finding.description: In patients with negative landmark MRD and EGFR/ALK wild-type tumors, there was no significant difference in DFS with or without adjuvant immunotherapy
finding.effect_summary: No DFS benefit from adjuvant immunotherapy in MRD-negative EGFR/ALK wild-type patients (P=0.534)

source.title: Potential of Minimal Residual Disease in Guiding Adjuvant Therapy Decisions in Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer.

finding.description: Clearance of ctDNA during adjuvant therapy was associated with better prognosis than persistently detected ctDNA
finding.effect_summary: ctDNA clearance during therapy associated with better prognosis (P<0.001)

source.title: Radiogenomic approach combining CT-based radiomics and liquid biopsy improves prognostic stratification in patients with advanced NSCLC.

finding.description: Overall concordance between tissue and ctDNA molecular profiling in advanced NSCLC patients
finding.effect_summary: 85% concordance between tissue and ctDNA profiling

source.title: Radiogenomic approach combining CT-based radiomics and liquid biopsy improves prognostic stratification in patients with advanced NSCLC.

finding.description: KRAS mutation concordance between tissue and ctDNA in NSCLC
finding.effect_summary: 99% concordance for KRAS mutations (Kappa 0.97)

source.title: Radiogenomic approach combining CT-based radiomics and liquid biopsy improves prognostic stratification in patients with advanced NSCLC.

finding.description: Combined clinical-radiomic-genetic model performance for overall survival prediction
finding.effect_summary: C-index 0.73 for combined model vs 0.62 for clinical-only

source.title: Radiogenomic approach combining CT-based radiomics and liquid biopsy improves prognostic stratification in patients with advanced NSCLC.

finding.description: Radiomic score as independent predictor of overall survival
finding.effect_summary: Radiomic score independently predicts OS (p<0.001)

source.title: Radiogenomic approach combining CT-based radiomics and liquid biopsy improves prognostic stratification in patients with advanced NSCLC.

finding.description: Presence of actionable alterations as independent predictor of overall survival
finding.effect_summary: Actionable alterations independently predict OS (p=0.041)

source.title: Radiogenomic approach combining CT-based radiomics and liquid biopsy improves prognostic stratification in patients with advanced NSCLC.

finding.description: Integrated model performance for disease-free survival prediction
finding.effect_summary: C-index 0.77 for integrated model vs 0.59 for clinical-only

source.title: Radiogenomic approach combining CT-based radiomics and liquid biopsy improves prognostic stratification in patients with advanced NSCLC.

finding.description: Baseline ctDNA detection associated with higher disease progression risk in EGFR-mutant NSCLC
finding.effect_summary: Detectable baseline ctDNA predicts higher progression risk (p=0.018)

source.title: Radiogenomic approach combining CT-based radiomics and liquid biopsy improves prognostic stratification in patients with advanced NSCLC.

finding.description: Combined clinical-radiogenomic model performance in EGFR-mutant NSCLC subgroup
finding.effect_summary: C-index 0.80 for combined model in EGFR-mutant subgroup

source.title: Radiogenomic approach combining CT-based radiomics and liquid biopsy improves prognostic stratification in patients with advanced NSCLC.

finding.description: ctDNA molecular clearance rate at first follow-up during targeted therapy
finding.effect_summary: 17 of 21 patients achieved ctDNA molecular clearance

source.title: A Retrospective, Multicenter Analysis Within the National Network Genomic Medicine Lung Cancer in Germany to Detect RET Fusions as a Possible Mechanism of Resistance in Patients With EGFR Mutations.

finding.description: Nine patients with EGFR-mutated NSCLC and co-occurring RET fusions were identified, with median age 54 years
finding.effect_summary: 9 patients with EGFR-mutated NSCLC and RET fusions identified

source.title: A Retrospective, Multicenter Analysis Within the National Network Genomic Medicine Lung Cancer in Germany to Detect RET Fusions as a Possible Mechanism of Resistance in Patients With EGFR Mutations.

finding.description: RET fusions were detected at diagnosis in 2 patients and acquired in 7 patients during EGFR-TKI treatment
finding.effect_summary: RET fusions present at diagnosis (n=2) or acquired during treatment (n=7)

source.title: A Retrospective, Multicenter Analysis Within the National Network Genomic Medicine Lung Cancer in Germany to Detect RET Fusions as a Possible Mechanism of Resistance in Patients With EGFR Mutations.

finding.description: Median time to detection of acquired RET fusions during EGFR-TKI treatment was 11.4 months
finding.effect_summary: Median time to acquired RET fusion detection: 11.4 months

source.title: A Retrospective, Multicenter Analysis Within the National Network Genomic Medicine Lung Cancer in Germany to Detect RET Fusions as a Possible Mechanism of Resistance in Patients With EGFR Mutations.

finding.description: The most common EGFR mutation was exon 19 deletion in 6 patients, followed by L858R in 1 patient and exon 20 insertions in 2 patients
finding.effect_summary: EGFR mutations: exon 19 del (n=6), L858R (n=1), exon 20 ins (n=2)

source.title: A Retrospective, Multicenter Analysis Within the National Network Genomic Medicine Lung Cancer in Germany to Detect RET Fusions as a Possible Mechanism of Resistance in Patients With EGFR Mutations.

finding.description: Six patients treated with third-generation EGFR-TKIs in first-line had median PFS of 13.5 ± 9.2 months
finding.effect_summary: Median PFS with 3rd-gen EGFR-TKIs: 13.5 ± 9.2 months (n=6)

source.title: A Retrospective, Multicenter Analysis Within the National Network Genomic Medicine Lung Cancer in Germany to Detect RET Fusions as a Possible Mechanism of Resistance in Patients With EGFR Mutations.

finding.description: One patient treated with second-generation EGFR-TKI afatinib in first-line had PFS of 8.7 months
finding.effect_summary: PFS with afatinib: 8.7 months (n=1)

source.title: A Retrospective, Multicenter Analysis Within the National Network Genomic Medicine Lung Cancer in Germany to Detect RET Fusions as a Possible Mechanism of Resistance in Patients With EGFR Mutations.

finding.description: Four patients treated with chemo-immunotherapy in second-line had median PFS of 6.5 ± 2.5 months
finding.effect_summary: Median PFS with chemo-immunotherapy: 6.5 ± 2.5 months (n=4)

source.title: A Retrospective, Multicenter Analysis Within the National Network Genomic Medicine Lung Cancer in Germany to Detect RET Fusions as a Possible Mechanism of Resistance in Patients With EGFR Mutations.

finding.description: One patient treated with chemotherapy alone in second-line had PFS of 0.3 months
finding.effect_summary: PFS with chemotherapy: 0.3 months (n=1)

source.title: A Retrospective, Multicenter Analysis Within the National Network Genomic Medicine Lung Cancer in Germany to Detect RET Fusions as a Possible Mechanism of Resistance in Patients With EGFR Mutations.

finding.description: Three patients treated with combined osimertinib and pralsetinib had PFS ranging from 3.9 to 10.5 months
finding.effect_summary: PFS with combined osimertinib+pralsetinib: 3.9-10.5 months (n=3)

source.title: A Retrospective, Multicenter Analysis Within the National Network Genomic Medicine Lung Cancer in Germany to Detect RET Fusions as a Possible Mechanism of Resistance in Patients With EGFR Mutations.

finding.description: Median overall survival for the entire cohort was 27 months with 4 patients still alive at last follow-up
finding.effect_summary: Median OS: 27 months (range 7 to >30 months), 4 patients alive

source.title: Beyond mutations: epigenetic and fragmentomic landscapes of cfDNA in lung cancer.

finding.description: cfDNA epigenetic and fragmentomic analyses represent a transformative frontier in lung cancer diagnostics and monitoring with significant potential
finding.effect_summary: cfDNA epigenetic and fragmentomic analyses show transformative potential in lung cancer diagnostics

source.title: Beyond mutations: epigenetic and fragmentomic landscapes of cfDNA in lung cancer.

finding.description: Most cfDNA epigenetic and fragmentomic studies are limited by modest cohort sizes and reports of survival benefits, requiring large-scale validation
finding.effect_summary: Current studies limited by small cohorts, need large-scale validation
